completions.label: ng-component-class

documentation.value: Creates a standalone Angular component with template, styles, and lifecycle hook.

completions.label: ng-service-class

documentation.value: Creates an Angular injectable service with root provider.

completions.label: ng-directive-class

documentation.value: Creates a standalone Angular directive.

completions.label: ng-pipe-class

documentation.value: Creates a standalone Angular pipe with transform method.

completions.label: ng-guard-fn

documentation.value: Creates an Angular functional route guard using CanActivateFn.

completions.label: ng-interceptor-fn

documentation.value: Creates an Angular functional HTTP interceptor.

completions.label: ng-module-class

documentation.value: Creates an Angular NgModule with declarations, imports, providers, and exports.

completions.label: ng-resolver-fn

documentation.value: Creates an Angular functional route resolver.

completions.label: ng-feature-module

documentation.value: Creates an Angular feature module with its own routing module.
Creates a lazy-loaded route using loadComponent for standalone components.

completions.label: ng-signal-component

documentation.value: Creates an Angular component using signals for state management.

completions.label: ng-reactive-form

documentation.value: Creates an Angular component with a reactive form using FormBuilder.

completions.label: ng-template-form

documentation.value: Creates an Angular component with a template-driven form using ngModel.

completions.label: @Component

documentation.value: Marks a class as an Angular component and supplies configuration metadata that determines how the component should be processed, instantiated, and used at runtime.
Marks a class as an NgModule and supplies configuration metadata.
Marks a class as available to be provided and injected as a dependency.
Marks a class as an Angular directive and provides configuration metadata.
Marks a class as an Angular pipe and supplies configuration metadata.
Marks a class field as an input property. The input property is bound to a DOM property in the template.
Marks a class field as an output property and supplies configuration metadata.
Configures a view query. The change detector looks for the first element or directive matching the selector in the view DOM.
Configures a view query. The change detector looks for all elements or directives matching the selector in the view DOM.
Configures a content query. The change detector looks for the first element or directive matching the selector in the content DOM.
Configures a content query. The change detector looks for all elements or directives matching the selector in the content DOM.
Marks a DOM property or element attribute as a host-binding property and supplies configuration metadata.
Declares a host listener. Angular will invoke the decorated method when the host element emits the specified event.
Specifies a custom provider of a dependency using an injection token.
Marks a dependency as optional. The injector provides null if the dependency is not found.
Instructs the DI framework to resolve a dependency only from the local injector.
Instructs the DI framework to resolve a dependency starting from the parent injector.
Instructs the DI framework to resolve a dependency by checking the host element's injector.
Decorator that marks a class as an Angular component with template, styles, and metadata.
Interface for the ngOnInit lifecycle hook. Called once after the first ngOnChanges.
Interface for the ngOnDestroy lifecycle hook. Called once when the directive is destroyed.
Interface for the ngOnChanges lifecycle hook. Called when any data-bound input property changes.
Interface for the ngAfterViewInit lifecycle hook. Called after the component's view has been fully initialized.
Interface for the ngAfterContentInit lifecycle hook. Called after content has been projected into the component.
Interface for the ngDoCheck lifecycle hook. Called during every change detection run.
Interface for the ngAfterViewChecked lifecycle hook. Called after every check of the component's view.
Interface for the ngAfterContentChecked lifecycle hook. Called after every check of projected content.
Decorator that marks a class as an NgModule and supplies configuration metadata.
Decorator that marks a class as available to be provided and injected as a dependency.
Decorator that marks a class as an Angular directive.
Decorator that marks a class as a pipe and supplies configuration metadata.
Use in directives and components to emit custom events synchronously or asynchronously.
Defines the strategy that the default change detector uses. OnPush means the change detector runs only when inputs change or events fire.
Sets the change detection strategy to OnPush, improving performance by only checking when inputs change.
Defines the template and style encapsulation options available for a component.
Base class that provides change detection functionality. A change-detection tree collects all views that are to be checked for changes.
A wrapper around a native element inside a View. Use with caution as direct DOM access bypasses Angular's templating.
Represents an embedded template that can be used to instantiate embedded views.
Represents a container where one or more views can be attached to a component.
Extend this base class to implement custom rendering. Provides a safe way to manipulate DOM elements.
An injectable service for executing work inside or outside of the Angular zone. Optimizes change detection.
Concrete injectors implement this interface. The Injector is responsible for retrieving instances of dependencies.
Allows registering cleanup callbacks that are invoked when the associated context is destroyed.
Injects a token from the currently active injector. Must be used in an injection context.
Interface that all pipes must implement. Defines a transform method.
An unmodifiable list of items that Angular keeps up to date when the state of the application changes.
A hashtable of changes represented by SimpleChange objects stored at the declared property name.
Creates a token that can be used in a DI Provider. Use an InjectionToken whenever the type is not sufficient to identify a dependency.
Creates a writable signal with an initial value. Signals are reactive primitives that notify consumers when they change.
Creates a memoized signal which derives its value from other signals. Re-evaluates only when dependencies change.
Creates an effect that runs a side-effectful function whenever one of the signals it reads changes.
Declares a signal-based input property. Alternative to @Input decorator with signal semantics.
Declares a required signal-based input property. The parent must provide a value.
Declares a signal-based output property. Alternative to @Output decorator.
Creates a model signal for two-way data binding. Combines input and output in a single API.
Creates a required model signal for two-way data binding.
Signal-based alternative to @ViewChild. Returns a signal that updates when the queried element changes.
Signal-based required view child query. Throws if the element is not found.
Signal-based alternative to @ViewChildren. Returns a signal with a list of matching elements.
Signal-based alternative to @ContentChild. Queries projected content.
Signal-based alternative to @ContentChildren. Queries all matching projected content.
Creates a writable signal that resets to a computed value whenever a source signal changes.
Creates a resource that loads data asynchronously based on a reactive request signal.
Converts an Observable to a signal. Values emitted by the Observable update the signal.
Converts a signal to an Observable that emits whenever the signal value changes.
New built-in control flow for conditional rendering. Replaces *ngIf.
Conditional rendering with else branch.
New built-in control flow for repeating elements. Replaces *ngFor. Requires a track expression.
New built-in control flow for switch/case rendering. Replaces ngSwitch.
Defers loading of a section of the template until a trigger condition is met. Supports lazy loading.
Declares a local template variable. Useful for caching complex expressions.
Tracks the value and validation status of an individual form control.
Tracks the value and validity state of a group of FormControl instances.
Creates an AbstractControl from a user-specified configuration. Reduces boilerplate for creating forms.
Tracks the value and validity state of an array of FormControl, FormGroup, or FormArray instances.
Provides a set of built-in validators: required, minLength, maxLength, pattern, email, min, max, etc.
Validator that requires the control to have a non-empty value.
Validator that requires the control to have a value that passes email validation.
Validator that requires the control value to have a minimum length.
Validator that requires the control value to have a maximum length.
Validator that requires the control value to match a regex pattern.
Module that provides the directives and providers for reactive forms: formControl, formGroup, formArrayName.
Module that provides NgModel and related directives for template-driven forms.
Two-way data binding using ngModel. Requires FormsModule to be imported.
Adds router directives and providers. Use forRoot for root module and forChild for feature modules.
A service that provides navigation among views and URL manipulation capabilities.
Provides access to information about a route associated with a component loaded in an outlet.
Acts as a placeholder that Angular dynamically fills based on the current router state.
Turns user clicks into router navigations. Can be applied to anchor tags or any element.
Import for standalone components to use routerLink directive.
Tracks whether the linked route of an element is currently active and sets CSS classes.
An array of Route objects used to configure the router.
Interface for a class-based guard that determines if a route can be activated.
Interface for a guard that determines if the user can navigate away from a route.
Interface for a class that resolves data before a route is activated.
Sets up providers for the router. Used in standalone application configuration.
Enables binding route parameters directly to component inputs.
Performs HTTP requests. Available as an injectable class with methods to perform HTTP requests.
Represents the header configuration options for an HTTP request. Instances are immutable.
An HTTP request/response body that represents serialized parameters. Instances are immutable.
Interface for intercepting outgoing HTTP requests and incoming responses.
Sets up providers for HttpClient. Used in standalone application configuration.
Configures functional HTTP interceptors for the application.
Configures HttpClient to use the Fetch API instead of XMLHttpRequest.
A structural directive that conditionally includes a template based on the value of an expression.
A structural directive that renders a template for each item in a collection.
A structural directive that adds or removes templates when the host expression matches the switch expression.
Adds and removes CSS classes on an HTML element.
Updates styles of the host HTML element.
Subscribes to an Observable or Promise and returns the latest value emitted. Automatically unsubscribes on destroy.
Formats a date value according to locale rules.
Transforms a number to a currency string according to locale rules.
Transforms a number into a decimal string according to locale rules.
Transforms a number to a percentage string according to locale rules.
Converts a value into its JSON-format representation. Useful for debugging.
Creates a new Array or String containing a subset of the elements.
Transforms text to all upper case.
Transforms text to all lower case.
Transforms text to title case (first letter of each word capitalized).
Transforms Object or Map into an array of key-value pairs.
Marks a component, directive, or pipe as standalone. Standalone components don't need to be declared in an NgModule.
Collects providers from NgModules for use in standalone application configuration.
Provides NgZone-based change detection for the application.
Set of config options available during the application bootstrap operation.
Bootstraps an instance of a standalone Angular component as the root of the application.
Called once after the first ngOnChanges. Initialize the directive/component here.
Called once when the directive/component is destroyed. Cleanup subscriptions and detach event handlers here.
Called when any data-bound input property changes. Receives a SimpleChanges object.
Called after the component's view and child views have been fully initialized.
Called after content projection into the component is complete.
Called during every change detection run. Use to detect changes Angular won't catch on its own.
Called after every check of the component's view.
Called after every check of projected content.
Type for functional route guards. Replaces class-based CanActivate.
Type for functional deactivation guards.
Type for functional route resolvers.
Type for functional HTTP interceptors.
Exports all the basic Angular directives and pipes, such as NgIf, NgFor, DecimalPipe, etc.
Adds OnInit interface and ngOnInit method to the class.
Adds OnDestroy interface and ngOnDestroy method to the class.
Sends a GET request to a specified URL and returns an Observable.
Sends a POST request to a specified URL and returns an Observable.
Sends a PUT request to a specified URL and returns an Observable.
Sends a DELETE request to a specified URL and returns an Observable.
Sends a PATCH request to a specified URL and returns an Observable.
Disables CSS encapsulation. Styles apply globally.
Default. Emulates native Shadow DOM by adding unique attributes to host elements and styles.
Uses the browser's native Shadow DOM for true style encapsulation.
Uses the default CheckAlways strategy, checking the component every change detection cycle.
Operator that completes an Observable when the associated component or service is destroyed.
Sets up providers for Angular animations.
Sets up providers for lazy-loaded Angular animations.
